dashboard.title: Overview
dashboard.body:
Status at a glance: key indicators, open deviations and incidents.
What to do: scan the big picture; for root-cause analysis go to Cockpit, to work problems go to Incidents.

cockpit.title: Cockpit — decision-making
cockpit.body:
Your workspace for the loop: notice → understand → decide → act → learn. The system clusters deviations into Situations and shows the forecast, recommendations and running processes.
What to do: pick a situation → review the root-cause hypothesis and forecast → accept a recommendation (it starts the process).

map.title: Map
map.body:
Geography by region: where indicators left their corridor.
What to do: click a region for details.

metrics.title: Metrics — explorer
metrics.body:
Charts of raw metric values over time and dimensions — for data exploration.
What to do: pick a metric and slice. Thresholds and indicators are set in Settings → Indicators.

alerts.title: Deviations
alerts.body:
An indicator is outside its normal corridor RIGHT NOW — a 'firing' signal.
What to do: acknowledge / take it, or go to Cockpit where deviations are clustered into situations.

incidents.title: Incidents
incidents.body:
Tickets to work (synced with i-doit). Opened on chronic deviations or manually.
What to do: assign, change status, escalate per SLA.

settings.title: Settings
settings.body:
Configure the system: data sources, indicators and corridors, playbooks and processes, notifications, SLA, escalation.
Start with Data sources (where data comes from) → Indicators (what counts as normal).

dataSources.title: Data sources
dataSources.body:
Where metrics come from: host agent, HTTP pull/push, Kafka.
What to do: add a source — metrics start flowing into the catalog and charts.

indicators.title: Indicators
indicators.body:
Goal → indicator → factors. An indicator has a normal 'corridor'; leaving it = a deviation.
What to do: attach metrics, set corridor bounds and the chronicle threshold.

metricsCatalog.title: Metric catalog
metricsCatalog.body: Reference of metrics: names, units, default thresholds. Actual values come from Data sources.

dimensions.title: Dimensions
dimensions.body: Data slices (region, service, DC...) and their allowed values — used to filter metrics.

playbooks.title: Playbooks — knowledge base
playbooks.body:
'What to do' on a deviation: triggers + a hint checklist + a link to a Process (executable).
The system offers matching playbooks as recommendations in the Cockpit.

rules.title: Rules (rule engine)
rules.body: Conditions on metrics → alert. Mostly superseded by indicators/corridors; use for one-off rules.

mlConfigs.title: ML configs
mlConfigs.body: Forecast and anomaly-detection settings per metric (Prophet etc.).

notifications.title: Notification channels
notifications.body: Where to send alerts: Telegram, e-mail, webhook. Per tenant.

escalation.title: Escalation chains
escalation.body: Who to escalate an incident to, and after how long, if no one responded.

sla.title: SLA policies
sla.body: Response and resolution deadlines by priority. Breaches are computed against these.

dependencies.title: Indicator dependencies
dependencies.body: A 'what depends on what' graph. Deviations are clustered into situations and root cause is found along it.

admin.title: Administration
admin.body: Users, roles and permissions, tenants, audit log. For administrators.

users.title: Users
users.body: Accounts, roles and a user's tenant. You manage users in your own tenant.

roles.title: Roles & permissions (RBAC)
roles.body: What a role is allowed to do. Permissions are carried in the user's token.

tenants.title: Tenants
tenants.body: Tenant organizations. Tenant management is only from the 'default' tenant (super-admin).

audit.title: Audit log
audit.body: Who did what: logins, changes, escalations, syncs. For review and compliance.

cockpit.situationsEmptyHint: Situations form by correlating linked indicators. Define dependencies in Settings → Dependencies so simultaneous deviations group into a situation.
cockpit.selectForForecast: Select an alert to see the forecast
cockpit.checklistIncomplete: Not all checklist items are done
cockpit.scenarioNeedsAssumption: Provide a name and at least one assumption

settingsDss.name: Name
settingsDss.processHint: The formal process: steps with assignees and SLA. Started when the recommendation is accepted.
settingsDss.checklistHint: A hint shown in the recommendation card, NOT executable steps. Formal steps with roles and SLA are defined by the process above.

metrics.empty: Select a metric and click Forecast to see predictions

settingsDep.title: Indicator dependencies
settingsDep.hint: Links between indicators feed correlation: when linked indicators deviate at the same time the engine groups them into a single "Situation" (one root cause instead of scattered alerts). Without dependencies the cockpit "Situations" block stays empty.
settingsDep.noSelf: An indicator cannot depend on itself

notif.name: Name
notif.eventsHint: Which events to send. 'all' = every event.

dataSrc.hint: Where the system gets metrics from. The collector and kafka consumer read enabled sources from this registry.
dataSrc.name: Name
dataSrc.keyAutogen: Leave empty — a key is generated and shown once
dataSrc.keyOnce: API key created — copy it now (it won't be shown again)
dataSrc.pushHint: Agents POST to {{path}} with an X-API-KEY header.
